human_verification.notes: Reviewed cannabis-specific traceability boundaries, jurisdiction and system-of-record dependence, manifest and inventory-adjustment evidence handling, COA and remediation disclosure posture, scenario-to-ability coverage, and refusal of legal, tax-signoff, product-release, and regulator-facing authority.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and cross-industry research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus cross-industry are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to cannabis reporting, evidence handling, gap analysis, and escalation support only.
Authority sources cover jurisdiction-specific reporting systems, recordkeeping, manifests, reconciliation, testing evidence, COA access, and remediation disclosure handling.
Scenario set covers missing evidence, conflicting policy or regulator sources, legal or signoff requests, tenant fact gaps, adjacent-lane routing, and cannabis-specific refusal behavior.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; jurisdiction and system-of-record differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
